data.title: Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts Give Their Take On 3 Utilities Stocks Delivering High-Dividend Yields
data.description:
During times of turbulence and uncertainty in the markets, many investors turn to dividend-yielding stocks. These are often companies that have high free cash flows and reward shareholders with a high dividend payout.

Benzinga readers can review the latest analyst takes on their favorite stocks by visiting Analyst Stock Ratings page. Traders can sort through Benzinga's extensive database of analyst ratings, including by analyst accuracy.

Below are the ratings of the most accurate analysts for three high-yielding stocks in the utilities sector.

Edison International (NYSE:EIX)

  • Dividend Yield: 5.78%
  • Barclays analyst Nicholas Campanella maintained an Overweight rating and cut the price target from $68 to $67 on Jan. 22, 2026. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 65%.
  • Wells Fargo analyst Shahriar Pourreza downgraded the stock from Equal-Weight to Underweight and raised the price target from $56 to $59 on Jan. 20, 2026. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 67%.
  • Recent News: On Oct. 28, Edison International posted upbeat quarterly results.
  • Benzinga Pro’s real-time newsfeed alerted to latest EIX news.

Brookfield Infrastructure Partners L.P. (NYSE:BIP)

  • Dividend Yield: 4.88%
  • Jefferies analyst Anthony Linton maintained a Buy rating with a price target of $35 on Aug. 29, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 50%.
  • RBC Capital analyst Maurice Choy maintained an Outperform rating with a price target of $40 on March 25, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 51%
  • Recent News: Brookfield Infrastructure Partners said it will hold its fourth quarter conference call on Thursday, Jan. 29.
  • Benzinga Pro's real-time newsfeed alerted to latest BIP news.

Dominion Energy Inc (NYSE:D)

  • Dividend Yield: 4.48%
  • Barclays analyst Nicholas Campanella maintained an Overweight rating and cut the price target from $64 to $63 on Jan. 22, 2026. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 65%.
  • Wells Fargo analyst Shahriar Pourreza maintained an Overweight rating and lowered the price target from $67 to $64 on Jan. 20, 2026. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 67%.
  • Recent News: Dominion Energy said it will host its fourth-quarter earnings call on Monday, Feb. 23.
  • Benzinga Pro’s real-time newsfeed alerted to latest D news

data.title: Apple Has 27% Upside, JPMorgan Says, Even As Memory Costs Surge And App Store Growth Slows
data.description:
In anticipation of Apple Inc.‘s (NASDAQ:AAPL) fiscal first-quarter earnings report, JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for the tech giant.

Growth Drivers Beyond AppStore

JPMorgan raised its price target on Apple to $315 from $305, citing confidence in the company's outlook, and reaffirmed its overweight rating on the stock, reported CNBC on Monday.

The bank’s analyst, Samik Chatterjee, cited better-than-expected iPhone demand and a reduction in operating expenses as potential drivers for an earnings beat. Chatterjee believes that these factors could lead to a 27% upside from the current stock price.

Despite Apple’s recent underperformance, JPMorgan views this as an attractive entry point. The analyst is optimistic about the company’s future, particularly given the upcoming iPhone 17 demand, despite investor worries over rising memory costs, possible pricing sensitivity affecting demand, and slightly weaker growth in App Store services.

Chatterjee expects robust iPhone 17 demand and reduced operating costs to help Apple deliver an earnings and revenue beat this Thursday. He expects Apple's Services revenue to grow 7% year over year, below the 14% guidance, but noted the company has multiple growth drivers beyond the App Store that could support stronger long-term performance

Apple Set For Record Q1 On iPhone Strength

Earlier in January,  Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan predicted that Apple could break first-quarter records despite looming memory costs. Mohan expects first-quarter revenue to beat estimates, driven by strong iPhone sales and double-digit growth in Services. He estimates 85 million iPhones were sold in the quarter, raises second-quarter forecasts, and sees continued strong demand for the iPhone 17. iPhone revenue is projected to rise 17% year over year, marking one of the company's strongest quarters ever.

This news comes on the heels of reports that Apple is rekindling its chip partnership with Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) for its non-Pro iPhone models. This move is seen as a significant shift in Apple’s supply chain strategy and could potentially impact the company’s future performance.

Apple is set to report its first-quarter results on Jan. 29.

Benzinga’s Edge Rankings place Apple in the 90th percentile for quality and the 67th percentile for momentum, reflecting its average performance. Benzinga's screener allows you to compare Apple's performance with its peers.  

Price Action: Over the past year, Apple stock climbed 7.91%, as per data from Benzinga Pro. On Friday, the stock edged 0.12% lower to close at $248.04.

Photo: hanohiki via Shutterstock

data.title: Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, SanDisk And More: Stocks With Earnings This Week
data.description:
Earnings season is in full swing with reports this week from three of the so-called “Magnificent Seven” names — Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA), Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) — plus defense primes, telecoms and more. 

Read on for the highlights of this week's earnings calendar, analyst estimates and key metrics to watch. 

  • TSLA stock is moving ahead of earnings. See the chart and price action here.

Monday, Jan. 26

After Market Close:

Monday starts off fairly quietly, with only a few companies on the radar after the closing bell:

  • Nucor Corp. (NYSE:NUE)
  • AGNC Investment Corp. (NASDAQ:AGNC)
  • Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO)

Tuesday, Jan. 27

Before Market Open:

The spotlight will be on UnitedHealth Group Inc. (NYSE:UNH) when it releases Q4 results ahead of Tuesday's opening bell. Analysts expect the health care giant to report earnings of $2.10 per share on revenue of a whopping $113.82 billion, according to estimates from Benzinga Pro. 

Investors will be watching UnitedHealth's medical care ratio (MCR) to determine if elevated utilization in outpatient and physician services has begun to stabilize. 

Attention will also be focused on management’s 2026 financial guidance, specifically regarding the path to margin recovery and the impact of the company’s strategic decision to rebate Affordable Care Act profits.

The week gets cranking on Tuesday morning with the following companies also reporting before the market open: 

  • United Parcel Service, Inc. (NYSE:UPS)
  • Boeing Co. (NYSE:BA)
  • NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE)
  • RTX Corp. (NYSE:RTX)
  • American Airlines Group Inc. (NASDAQ:AAL)
  • General Motors Co. (NYSE:GM)
  • Union Pacific Corp. (NYSE:UNP)
  • Northrop Grumman Corp. (NYSE:NOC)
  • HCA Healthcare, Inc. (NYSE:HCA)
  • JetBlue Airways Corp. (NASDAQ:JBLU)

After Close:

Reporting after Tuesday's closing bell will be:

  • Texas Instruments Inc. (NASDAQ:TXN)
  • Seagate Technology Holdings Plc (NASDAQ:STX)
  • Nextpower Inc. (NASDAQ:NXT)

Wednesday, Jan. 28

Before Market Open:

ASML Holding N.V. (NASDAQ:ASML) is set to release its fourth-quarter results ahead of Wednesday's opening bell. Wall Street is looking for earnings of $8.86 per share on revenue of $11.11 billion, according to Benzinga Pro.

GE Vernova Inc. (NYSE:GEV) will also release its results on Wednesday morning, with analysts expecting EPS of $3 on revenue of $10.21 billion for the quarter. 

Also reporting on Wednesday morning are: 

  • Starbucks Corp. (NASDAQ:SBUX)
  • AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T)
  • MSCI Inc. (NYSE:MSCI)
  • Automatic Data Processing, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADP)
  • Amphenol Corp. (NYSE:APH)
  • Progressive Corp. (NYSE:PGR)
  • Corning Inc. (NYSE:GLW)
  • Elevance Health, Inc. (NYSE:ELV)
  • General Dynamics Corp. (NYSE:GD)
  • Danaher Corp. (NYSE:DHR)

After Market Close:

Three Mag 7 members are reporting after Wednesday's closing bell:

  • Tesla investors will focus on whether gross margins have stabilized or continue to slide under pressure from Chinese competition and the expiration of federal EV tax credits. The market will also be looking for concrete timelines for the cybercab robotaxi and updates on the transition of full self-driving (FSD) to a subscription-only model.
  • Microsoft investors will prioritize Azure revenue growth to determine if AI demand is successfully driving cloud acceleration, The market will also closely scrutinize operating margins to evaluate whether aggressive AI capital expenditures and rising data-center energy costs are being balanced by efficiency gains.

The following companies will also report after Wednesday's closing bell:

  • ServiceNow, Inc. (NYSE:NOW)
  • International Business Machines Corp. (NYSE:IBM)
  • Lam Research Corp. (NASDAQ:LRCX)
  • Waste Management, Inc. (NYSE:WM)
  • Celestica Inc. (NYSE:CLS)
  • Fair Isaac Corp. (NYSE:FICO)
  • Southwest Airlines Co. (NYSE:LUV)
  • Levi Strauss & Co. (NYSE:LEVI)

Thursday, Jan. 29

Before Market Open:

Another jam-packed day of earnings kicks off with the following companies reporting before Thursday's market open:

  • Mastercard Inc. (NYSE:MA)
  • Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT)
  • Lockheed Martin Corp. (NYSE:LMT)
  • Altria Group, Inc. (NYSE:MO)
  • SAP SE (NYSE:SAP)
  • Honeywell International Inc. (NASDAQ:HON)
  • Blackstone Inc. (NYSE:BX)
  • Nasdaq, Inc. (NASDAQ:NDAQ)
  • Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (NYSE:TMO)
  • Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. (NYSE:RCL)
  • Comcast Corp. (NASDAQ:CMCSA)

After Market Close:

Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) will release its Q4 earnings after Thursday's closing bell, with analysts expecting EPS of $2.67 on revenue of $138.42 billion, according to estimates from Benzinga Pro. 

Investors will be focused on whether the iPhone 17 product cycle has triggered the long-awaited “super cycle.” Analysts will look for clarity on how surging memory prices and approximately $1.4 billion in quarterly tariffs are impacting gross margins despite record-breaking revenue expectations.

Speaking of surging memory prices, Western Digital Corp. (NASDAQ:WDC) and SanDisk Corp. (SNDK) will also report on Thursday afternoon. 

Investors can also expect reports from:

  • Visa Inc. (NYSE:V)
  • KLA Corp. (NASDAQ:KLAC)
  • Deckers Outdoor Corp. (NYSE:DECK)
  • Stryker Corp. (NYSE:SYK)

Friday, Jan. 30

Before Market Open:

Two of the largest U.S. oil companies, Exxon Mobil Corp. (NYSE:XOM) and Chevron Corp. (NYSE:CVX) will report on Friday morning. Investors will be listening for 2026 production guidance and any commentary regarding Venezuela following the removal of Nicolas Maduro.

The following companies will close out the week, reporting before Friday's opening bell: 

  • SoFi Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:SOFI)
  • American Express Co. (NYSE:AXP)
  • Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE:VZ)
  • Colgate-Palmolive Co. (NYSE:CL)
  • Canadian National Railway Co. (NYSE:CNI)
  • Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ:REGN)

Photo: iQoncept from Shutterstock

data.title: Caterpillar, Medtronic, Transocean And A Financial Stock: CNBC's 'Final Trades'
data.description:
On CNBC's “Halftime Report Final Trades,” Bryn Talkington, managing partner of Requisite Capital Management, said she is sticking with Capital One Financial Corporation (NYSE:COF).

On the earnings front, Capital One Financial, on Jan. 22, reported worse-than-expected fourth-quarter adjusted EPS results and announced it will acquire Brex.

Kevin Simpson, Capital Wealth Planning founder and CIO, said Medtronic plc (NYSE:MDT) has tons of free cash flow and improving margins.

Lending support to his choice, Bernstein analyst Lee Hambright, on Jan. 9, maintained Medtronic with an Outperform rating and raised the price target from $111 to $112, while Barclays analyst Matt Miksic, on Jan. 8, maintained the stock with an Overweight rating and boosted the price target from $111 to $116.

Don't forget to check out our premarket coverage here

Jim Lebenthal, partner at Cerity Partners, named Transocean Ltd. (NYSE:RIG) as his final trade.

Transocean is scheduled to report earnings for the fourth quarter and issue a fleet status report, after the closing bell on Thursday, Feb. 19.

Stephen Weiss, chief investment officer and managing partner of Short Hills Capital Partners, picked Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT) as his final trade.

Supporting his view, Wells Fargo analyst Jerry Revich, on Friday, maintained Caterpillar with an Overweight rating and raised the price target from $675 to $702.

Price Action:

  • Capital One Financial shares dipped 7.6% to close at $217.30 on Friday.
  • Medtronic fell 0.5% to settle at $100.88 during the session.
  • Transocean shares gained 2.8% to close at $4.83 on Friday.
  • Caterpillar shares fell 3.4% to close at $626.62 during the session.

Photo: Shutterstock
